meaning: n.<美口>(常供流浪者住的)廉价旅馆,小客栈
examples: At least one flophouse survives on the Bowery.
The pianist, he learned, was in his 40s and living in a flophouse.
He is now unemployed, and lives in a flophouse in Atlanta between a.
He is now unemployed, and lives in a flophouse in Atlanta between a jail and a strip club.
From the Chinese: chow-main, chop-suey, stir-fry, ketchup, flophouse, kowtow and tycoon.
